sanskrit:
विस्तरेणात्मनो योगं विभूतिं च जनार्दन।
भूयः कथय तृप्तिर्हि शृण्वतो नास्ति मेऽमृतम्॥
transliteration:
vistareṇātmano yogaṃ vibhūtiṃ ca janārdana |
bhūyaḥ kathaya tṛptir hi śṛṇvato nāsti me ’mṛtam ||
literal_meaning: In detail—of the Self—Yoga—and opulences—O Janārdana—again—speak—satisfaction—indeed—of the hearer—there is not—my—nectar.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna asks for an expanded narrative of Kṛṣṇa’s immanent presence because the very act of listening to this “nectar” produces inexhaustible psychological satiety; repetition deepens rather than depletes intrinsic motivation.
psychological_state: Flow-like absorption in a secure attachment figure; ego boundaries soften while agency remains intact.
trigger_scenario: A client finishing a powerful therapy session says, "I wish we had more time—your words feel like medicine I can’t overdose on."
anchor_text: Hearing divine presence never tires; each retelling deepens joy and satisfies the soul.
